participant.name: Dr Karim Benali
participant.persona_summary: Concise, efficient, slightly skeptical GP in Marseille

answers.Q4: Private group practice in an urban neighborhood
answers.Q7: I usually rely on HAS recommendations, Vidal, medical newsletters, and quick exchanges with GP colleagues when I need a second opinion.
answers.Q8: I keep an eye on innovation through podcasts, general practice newsletters, and what colleagues share in WhatsApp groups, but I do not actively chase every new tool.
answers.Q9: I mostly ask two senior GPs in my group and, for dermatology or pediatrics questions, the local specialists I refer to most often.
answers.Q10: I am fairly skeptical because many rep visits feel generic and disconnected from what I actually need in primary care.
answers.Q11: Clear comparative data, practical prescribing aids, and patient handouts that save me time during consultations.
answers.Q12: I am rarely available during the day; the only realistic options are a very short lunch meeting or an asynchronous digital update.
answers.Q13: Ideally it would be under 10 minutes, focused on one clinically relevant topic, with no marketing fluff and a concise digital recap afterward.
answers.Q14: I collaborate regularly with community pharmacists, especially around adherence, substitution questions, shortages, and checking whether patients understood treatment changes.
answers.Q15: Pharmacists are important because they often catch practical medication issues before I do and they help reinforce adherence with chronic patients.
answers.Q17: I would only want more collaboration if it is highly practical, transparent about evidence, and mostly digital rather than based on drop-in visits.
answers.Q19: It should get to the point faster, interrupt less when there is a pause, and make the transitions between topics feel more natural.
answers.Q20: For this kind of call, I would expect about 50 euros because it takes time out of clinic.
